_replace_note: Mock English exam-prep slide deck for Grade 12 learners. Shape: title + slides[]. Keep full-sentence bullets so friendly themes have enough text to render well.
title: English Exam Prep - Strategy and Core Skills (2026)

slides.title: Exam Overview
bullets: This mock deck gives a fast overview of the English exam format and major skill groups.
The goal is to build control over grammar, vocabulary, reading, and test timing.
Students should combine topic review with full timed practice to improve exam stamina.

slides.title: Question Types
bullets: Common sections include pronunciation, stress, grammar, vocabulary, error correction, and reading comprehension.
Reading tasks often require both detail scanning and broader inference about purpose or attitude.
Sentence transformation and paraphrase items test structure control rather than simple word memorisation.

slides.title: Time Management
bullets: Quick recognition items should take less time so more minutes remain for long reading passages.
If one question blocks you for too long, mark it and move on before returning later.
Reserve the final minutes to check the answer sheet, question numbers, and skipped items.

slides.title: Tense Control
bullets: Use time signals such as since, for, by the time, and recently to narrow the tense choice.
When two time points appear, decide which action happened first before selecting the verb form.
Contrast similar forms carefully, especially the past simple, present perfect, and past perfect.

slides.title: Conditionals
bullets: Type 1 describes real future possibility, while Type 2 describes an unreal present or future situation.
Type 3 focuses on unreal past results and usually includes had + V3 and would have + V3.
Mixed conditionals connect a past cause with a present result and require careful time logic.

slides.title: Passive Voice
bullets: A passive sentence needs the correct be form plus a past participle to match the original tense.
Modal passives follow the pattern modal + be + V3, while perfect passives use have been + V3.
Check whether the doer matters before keeping or removing the by-agent.

slides.title: Relative Clauses
bullets: Who and whom refer to people, which refers to things, and whose shows possession.
Where and when replace place and time phrases more naturally than repeating prepositions.
Reduced relative clauses use V-ing, V3, or to V depending on meaning and structure.

slides.title: Vocabulary Strategy
bullets: Learn words in collocations because natural combinations often decide the correct answer.
Group vocabulary by theme such as education, environment, technology, and public health.
When solving synonym or antonym items, anchor the meaning to the sentence context first.

slides.title: Reading Skills
bullets: Skim headings, topic sentences, and conclusion lines before answering detailed questions.
Scan for paraphrases instead of exact word matches because the passage often restates ideas.
For inference questions, choose the option that is supported indirectly rather than dramatically overstated.

slides.title: Final Review
bullets: Review your personal mistake log instead of trying to learn too many brand-new rules at the end.
Practice under real timing and classify mistakes by grammar, vocabulary, reading, or strategy.
Steady preparation, calm checking, and clear time control usually produce the best results.
